name: Destruction
tagline: Blast things apart with raw magical force.

baseAbility.name: Destructive Blast
baseAbility.description: Standard action. Melee or ranged touch attack (close range) dealing 1d6 bludgeoning damage per odd caster level (1d6 at CL1, 2d6 at CL3, 3d6 at CL5, ...). Subject to spell resistance. Spend 1 SP to bump the damage to one die per caster level instead.

notes: Only one (blast type) talent and one (blast shape) talent can modify any single blast. Blast types are grouped by theme (e.g. Fire Blast/Searing Blast share the Fire group) - this matters for talents that key off blast type groups.

talents.name: Admixture
talents.description: Combine two blast types on one blast (half damage each, effects of both apply). Costs an extra spell point and a slower casting time unless both blast types share a group.

talents.name: Cascade Failure
talents.description: Each blast that damages a creature stacks a -1 penalty on its saves against your blasts until your next turn.

talents.name: Clinging Blast
talents.description: Blast damage counts as ongoing for concentration-check purposes until your next turn (Fort negates).

talents.name: Crafted Blast
talents.description: An unmodified blast can deal bludgeoning, piercing, or slashing damage, and treats rolled 1s and 2s as 3s.

talents.name: Damage Control
talents.description: Blast can deal nonlethal damage instead, or deal no damage at all while keeping only minor cosmetic/secondary effects.

talents.name: Demolition
talents.description: +1 damage/die and full damage to objects (hardness still applies) when targeting inanimate objects; half damage to anything else struck this way.

talents.name: Energetic Response
talents.description: Use your blast to make attacks of opportunity as if it were a weapon.

talents.name: Epicenter
talents.description: You're immune to your own blast's damage and effects, including persistent shapes like Energy Wall.

talents.name: Extended Range
talents.description: Blast range increases one step (close -> medium -> long). Stackable.

talents.name: Focused Blast
talents.description: +1 damage/die on a blast with no (blast shape) talent applied.

talents.name: Gather Energy
talents.description: Slow the casting time by one step to reduce the blast's spell point cost by 1 (min 0).

talents.name: Selective Blast
talents.description: Exclude allies from an area blast (1 + 1 per 10 CL for free; spend 1 SP to exclude casting-modifier more).

talents.name: Spirit Blast
talents.description: Blast can affect ethereal/incorporeal targets normally, ignoring the usual 50% miss chance and damage halving.

talents.name: Wingbind
talents.description: Entangling blast types can also ground flying creatures on a failed extra Reflex save.

blastShapes.name: Blast Salvo
blastShapes.description: Split your blast into 2 touch-attack surges (dice divided as you like, min 1 die each); spend 1 SP for +1 surge, plus 1 more per 6 CL.

blastShapes.name: Blast Trap
blastShapes.description: Plant your blast as a hidden trap on an adjacent square; triggers on a creature stepping in (Reflex half), lasts 10 min/CL.

blastShapes.name: Chain Blast
blastShapes.description: Blast arcs between 1 + half your CL targets (min 2); stops the first time a target avoids damage.

blastShapes.name: Energy Sphere
blastShapes.description: Blast becomes a mobile 5-ft. sphere with a fly speed that you steer each round, damaging whoever it touches (Reflex negates).

blastShapes.name: Energy Strike
blastShapes.description: Deliver the blast through a weapon attack instead of a separate action.

blastShapes.name: Energy Tether
blastShapes.description: Ranged touch attack binds a target to you with a breakable tether that deals blast damage each round it holds (Fort half).

blastShapes.name: Energy Wall
blastShapes.description: Shape the blast into a wall (or, for 1 SP, a much larger wall/hemisphere) that damages anyone passing through it.

blastShapes.name: Explosive Orb
blastShapes.description: Blast becomes a burst centered anywhere in range (Reflex negates); spend 1 SP to expand the burst radius.

blastShapes.name: Guided Strike
blastShapes.description: +20 circumstance bonus to the blast's attack roll.

blastShapes.name: Rebuff
blastShapes.description: Use the blast defensively instead of offensively, granting a target evasion and cover bonuses against ranged attacks for a round.

blastShapes.name: Sculpt Blast
blastShapes.description: Blast becomes a cone, line, or self-centered burst with no attack roll needed (Reflex half).

blastTypes.name: Acid Blast
blastTypes.description: Deals acid damage; struck creatures also take 1 acid damage per die the following round.

blastTypes.name: Air Blast
blastTypes.description: Nonlethal bludgeoning damage plus a free ranged bull rush.

blastTypes.name: Electric Blast
blastTypes.description: Electricity damage; -3 AC/saves penalty against it for metal-armored or mostly-metal targets.

blastTypes.name: Fire Blast
blastTypes.description: Fire damage; failed Reflex save sets the target alight for ongoing fire damage.

blastTypes.name: Force Blast
blastTypes.description: Force damage; failed Reflex save knocks the target prone.

blastTypes.name: Frost Blast
blastTypes.description: Cold damage; failed Fort save staggers the target for a round.

blastTypes.name: Searing Blast
blastTypes.description: Fire damage using d8s instead of d6s, with no secondary effect.

blastTypes.name: Shock Blast
blastTypes.description: Electricity damage using d4s; failed Fort save dazes the target for a round.

blastTypes.name: Stone Blast
blastTypes.description: Physical damage type of your choice; ignores spell resistance and antimagic, and isn't treated as magical for DR purposes.

blastTypes.name: Thunder Blast
blastTypes.description: Sonic damage; failed Fort save deafens the target for 2d4 rounds.

blastTypes.name: Gloom Blast
blastTypes.description: Negative energy damage; sickens on a failed Fort save. Undead take no damage but are blinded instead.

blastTypes.name: Crystal Blast
blastTypes.description: Piercing damage using d4s; failed Reflex save entangles the target and creates difficult terrain that can be broken free of or destroyed.
